mechanism_narrative: A sustained high level of industrial production indicates ongoing economic expansion and capacity utilization near potential, which supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with INDPRO level over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, it signals labor market slack and economic weakness, reducing consumption and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of triggers, or negative returns are not significantly different from baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer prices and a federal funds rate below 1% produces negative real interest rates, which compresses discount rates and incentivizes investors to shift into equities as an inflation hedge, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL consecutive monthly increase and FEDFUNDS < 1.0
failure_condition: Real interest rate (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL YoY) does not fall when both conditions hold, or 20-day returns are not positive in a majority of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, elevated housing starts above 1.6 million units signal peaking demand and imminent oversupply, leading to a correction in residential investment and negative spillovers to equities over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggers, or inverse correlation between HOUST and subsequent returns is not statistically significant (p < 0.10) in a Chow test against early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of real retail and food services sales reflects robust consumer spending and aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS fails to predict positive forward returns; walk-forward Rank IC less than 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll employment exceeds 140 million, it indicates a very tight labor market with rising wages and consumer confidence, which boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days as sentiment rallies.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than half of the trigger instances, or the average return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous year-over-year increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic momentum with rising output and employment, reinforcing each other to drive corporate earnings and equity prices higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 60% of jointly triggered periods, or the combined signal does not outperform either series individually in out-of-sample Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 0.5%, it signals a severe economic downturn or financial crisis that has prompted emergency monetary easing, but equity markets interpret this as a sign of deep trouble, leading to negative forward returns over 20 trading days despite or because of the low rate.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggers, or the average return is not negative with statistical significance (one-sided t-test p < 0.05).
